text: The nitrate reduction reaction (NO3RR) has been studied as a potential route for the electrochemical synthesis of ammonia using less energy than the traditional Haber-Bosch process. Most studies are carried out at low nitrate concentrations, that is in the range of wastewater concentrations, and there is little information at the high nitrate concentrations of nuclear wastes and fertilizer production wastes. The use of concentrated electrolytes, with concentrations ≥ 1 M, in order to increase the production rate is also limited by the slow hydrogen transport to the electrode. Here, we show that a cocatalyst system consisting of a Ru/Cu2O catalyst is capable of producing ammonia at 10.0 A in 1 M nitrate in a 16 cm2 flow electrolyzer with 100% faradaic efficiency. The influence of hydrogens transfer to NO3− was investigated using deuterium labeling experiments and operando Fourier transform infrared (FTIR) spectroscopy over the Ru/Cu2O catalyst. By using these experiments along with ab initio molecular dynamics (AIMD) simulations we are able to investigate the mechanism of NO3RR in more detail.
